Keelhouse

lean macOS agent cockpit for Jason's real workflow — real CLI agents and composer first, with resizable explorer/editor/browser trays, without IDE bloat · 115 slices · 6 shipped

Open project folder -> talk to one or more real Claude/Codex/Gemini CLI agents in the main cockpit -> resize or move trays to inspect/edit files or preview web output -> switch projects without separate heavy VS Code windows. Build order is dependency-first: finish chrome/trays, package the native app, measure daily-driver value and resource use, then add settings, source-host connections, AI hooks, structured activity, and speculative expansion.

Terminal + processProjects + sessionsFiles + editorChrome + interactionQuality + shippingResearch & spikes🔒 launch blocker

Done

SREFERENCE-PRODUCT-AUDIT · Reference product auditResearch & spikes
v1

Lock which external products are useful references so Keelhouse does not drift into a VS Code clone or generic orchestrator.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-13: Zed remains the closest open-source chrome-quality reference, but Keelhouse translates that craft into an agent-first cockpit instead of copying an editor-first IDE. ConstellAgent, herdr, parallel-code, and Clave remain targeted workflow references; Orbit is useful for agent mechanics, not shell chrome. A live/package audit added Super Engineering as a closed-source reference for quiet persistent chrome, scoped settings, profiles, command sources, and worktree lifecycle policy. See docs/reference-products.md and docs/super-engineering-chrome-audit.md.

MAPPROOT · Promote app into real root/packageQuality + shipping
v0🔒

Turn the proven spike into the actual app package: stable naming, app identifier, scripts, dev/build paths, and repo layout. Stop treating the working terminal loop as a side experiment.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: app builds/runs from app/; npm package, Rust crate, and Tauri product identifier are renamed; README/ROADMAP point to app/; runtime smoke launched target/debug/agent-cli and spawned claude in the selected cwd.

MOPEN-FOLDER · Native open-folder workspaceProjects + sessions
v0🔒

Native folder picker creates the current workspace, matching the actual VS Code workflow: open app, pick Project A, terminal starts in Project A.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: first launch opens a native dialog; selected folder becomes the app workspace; terminal cwd matches it; Cmd+O switches folders.

MAGENT-LAUNCH · Launch real Claude in selected folderTerminal + process
v0🔒

Replace the current hardcoded login shell with a real agent process launched in the selected workspace. Start with Claude as the v0 default because it is the primary observed workflow.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: after folder selection/relaunch, one pane launches `claude` in that cwd from a persisted launch profile; Claude's TUI renders in the canvas.

SLAST-WORKSPACE · Persist and reopen last workspaceProjects + sessions
v0🔒

Quit/relaunch should not make the user pick the same project again. Store the last workspace path in a small local persistence layer.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08 for the current shell-pane default: relaunch reopens the last folder and starts the pane there. Recheck after AGENT-LAUNCH changes the default command.

MTERMINAL-HARDEN · Terminal component hardening passTerminal + process
v0

Close the remaining gaps between 'spike works' and 'can daily-drive one agent': resize behavior, scrollback basics, keyboard audit, copy/selection edge cases, and fast-output smoke tests.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: Ghostty viewport scrollback renders, Shift+PageUp/PageDown and wheel scrolling work, resize clamps dimensions, 300-line fast output stays usable, and copy-selection still works.

MPROCESS-ENV · Agent launch env and auth checksTerminal + process
v0🔒

Claude/Codex must launch with the same PATH/auth expectations as a normal terminal. Detect missing binaries, auth failures, and bad cwd before dumping the user into a dead pane.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: workspace cwd and command are preflighted before pane replacement; missing command renders a visible error with no child process; process exit emits a banner for runtime/auth-style failures; Claude still launches as a direct child in the selected cwd.

SDATA-STORAGE · Local state storage and resetProjects + sessions
v0

Define where workspace/session state lives and how to reset it when the app gets confused.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: docs/local-state.md documents the Tauri Store path, workspace.json schema, reversible reset command, and default launchProfile repair path.

MAPP-SHELL · Lean app shell: rail/editor/terminal layoutChrome + interaction
v0_5🔒

Build the shape that replaces VS Code for this workflow: left file rail, main editor area, bottom/right terminal pane area. No marketing page, no extra IDE chrome.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: one project opens into a stable three-part shell with left file rail surface, editor surface, and bottom terminal panel; terminal resize uses the measured terminal panel; build/tests pass; real Tauri smoke launched Claude in the selected cwd.

MFILE-RAIL · File explorer railFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

The VS Code feature Jason actually uses: a project tree on the left. Keep it dense, fast, and boring.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: backend lists the selected workspace with ignore::WalkBuilder, gitignore/app noisy-folder filtering, and an 8000-entry cap; frontend renders a dense React Arborist tree, directories expand/collapse, and files activate into the editor surface.

MFILE-WATCHER · File watcher and ignore rulesFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

The rail must reflect real project changes without drowning in node_modules, target, build output, or ignored files.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: debounced notify watcher emits workspace-tree-changed for relevant paths only; frontend refreshes the rail from list_workspace_tree; noisy paths are unit-tested; visual smoke showed a newly created top-level directory appear in the rail after launch.

SRECENT-PROJECTS · Recent projects switcherProjects + sessions
v0_5

Opening the same active projects repeatedly should be cheap, not a folder-picker ceremony every time.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: recentFolders persists, dedupes, caps, and prunes missing folders; the rail lists recent projects with an explicit Switch affordance; real Tauri smoke switched from Keelhouse to postures and persisted postures as the active folder.

LEDITOR · CodeMirror file editorFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

Open files from the rail into a real editor pane. This is not a decorative preview; it is the VS Code/Codex editor surface Jason actually uses.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: CodeMirror opens files from the rail with reliable pointer-down activation, line numbers, path context, TS/TSX/JS/JSX/MD syntax modes, dirty/saved status, Save button, and Cmd+S/Ctrl+S. Native UI smoke opened, edited, saved, and confirmed disk contents changed for a real workspace file.

MEDITOR-PARITY-UX · Robust editor parity UXFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

Match the coding editor orientation shown in the reference: dense file tree beside a real source editor with path context, gutter, dirty state, restored view position, and active-file reveal.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: editor shows breadcrumb path context, stable CodeMirror line gutter, selected file highlight, active-file reveal via React Arborist scrollTo, restored per-file selection/scroll state, dirty markers in editor and rail, and screenshot QA artifacts for selected, dirty, save-error, no-file, missing, and 1024x640 narrow states in docs/qa/editor-parity/.

SACTIVE-FILE-SYNC · Active file sync across rail and editorFiles + editor
v0_5

The user must always know which file is active. File rail selection, editor tab title, path bar, watcher refreshes, and workspace restore need to agree.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: activeFileByWorkspace persists by canonical workspace root; relaunch restores the saved file into rail selection, editor tab/path, and content; stale paths are cleared; watcher refresh reconciles the selected file to the fresh tree node when it still exists and preserves the missing-file warning when it disappears. Native screenshot proof: docs/qa/active-file-sync/restore.png.

SEDITOR-VIEW-STATE · Per-file editor view stateFiles + editor
v0_5

Switching files should not throw away the user's place. Cursor, selection, scroll, and focus are part of the editor experience.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: per-file view state captures selection anchor/head, scrollTop, and focus intent. Native smoke set first.ts to Ln 5, Col 9, switched to second.ts, switched back, restored the same line/column, then ArrowDown advanced to Ln 6, Col 1, proving CodeMirror focus was restored. Proof screenshots: docs/qa/editor-view-state/restore.png and docs/qa/editor-view-state/focus-restored.png.

MDIRTY-DRAFT-PROTECTION · Dirty draft protectionFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

The userflow audit found the main trust risk: unsaved work can be lost when changing files/projects. This must be blocked before the editor feels real.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: dirty file/workspace replacement opens a Save/Discard/Cancel modal with the file name and consequence. Save continues only after write_text_file succeeds; failed saves keep the draft and pending navigation. Covered by npm test flows for save failure, save success, discard, no pending navigation, prompt trigger logic, and modal copy; screenshot proof: docs/qa/dirty-draft-protection/modal.png.

SSAVE-ERROR-RECOVERY · Save failure recoveryFiles + editor
v0_5

Save failures need a recovery flow, not only a red message. The buffer must remain safe and the next action must be obvious.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: failed saves keep the edited buffer, show a scoped Save failed alert with the human error, expose Retry and Open externally, disable duplicate retry/save while a write is running, and clear the error only when the real write path succeeds. Covered by npm build/test and screenshot proof in docs/qa/editor-parity/save-error.png.

SEDITOR-RESPONSIVE-QA · Editor responsive screenshot QAQuality + shipping
v0_5

The userflow audit called out screenshot proof for editor states and small desktop widths before claiming parity.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: npm run qa:editor captures selected, dirty, save-error, missing-file, no-file, and 1024x640 narrow workbench states into docs/qa/editor-parity/. Visual audit confirmed clear rail/editor/terminal orientation with no overlapping chrome in narrow and no-file states.

SEDITOR-FIND-REPLACE · Editor find and replaceFiles + editor
v0_5

Basic file editing needs local find/replace; otherwise the editor is too thin to replace the VS Code surface Jason actually uses.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: CodeMirror search is included as a first-class editor extension with Cmd+F/F3/Cmd+G search keybindings, a visible Find button, and replace/replace-all support through the editor-native search panel. Screenshot proof: docs/qa/editor-parity/find-replace.png.

SEDITOR-LANGUAGE-MODES · Editor language modesFiles + editor
v0_5

The first editor pass needs credible syntax support for the file types this repo and Jason's projects use most.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: editor language extensions cover TypeScript, TSX/JSX, JavaScript, Markdown, HTML, CSS, Rust, JSON, TOML, YAML, and shell scripts/dotfiles. Covered by npm build and focused mode mapping tests.

MSAVE-CONFLICTS · External change and save conflict handlingFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

Agents and terminals can edit files while the editor is open. The app must not silently overwrite external changes.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: read_text_file returns modifiedMs, write_text_file receives the last-seen modifiedMs, and the Rust backend rejects stale writes before overwriting. The editor conflict surface offers Reload, Overwrite, and Open externally. Covered by cargo test stale-write rejection, npm build/test, and screenshot proof at docs/qa/editor-parity/save-conflict.png.

SLARGE-FILE-POLICY · Large and binary file policyFiles + editor
v0_5

The editor should fail safely on huge or binary files instead of freezing the app.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: backend rejects files over the 2 MB editor cap before full text load, rejects invalid UTF-8/binary files explicitly, and rejects oversized save buffers without touching disk. Covered by cargo tests for large read, binary read, and large save rejection.

MFILE-OPS · Basic file operationsFiles + editor
v0_5

Replacing the VS Code shell requires basic rail operations, not only open-file. These commands must be reachable from the rail, menu/shortcut paths, and the file context menu where relevant.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: create file, create folder, rename, delete, duplicate, and reveal in Finder work from the rail/context menu. Backend guards every mutation against escaping the workspace root; destructive operations confirm; dirty active files are protected. Covered by npm build/test, cargo test file-op guard/roundtrip coverage, and git diff --check.

SEDITOR-TABS · Open files and dirty-state tabsFiles + editor
v0_5

Let the user bounce between a few files without losing place. This is the minimum editor workflow, not a full IDE tab system.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: multiple open files appear as tabs with close controls; switching tabs preserves in-memory drafts; dirty files are marked in tabs and the rail; dirty tab close confirms before discard; rename/delete keeps open tabs aligned. Covered by npm build/test, editorTabs helper tests, screenshot QA, and visual selected/narrow inspection.

SAGENT-PROFILES · Agent command profilesTerminal + process
v0_5

Stop hardcoding one command forever. Add simple profiles for Codex, Gemini, Claude, and shell so the pane can launch the desired CLI without burning the wrong quota during testing.

Done criteria

Corrected 2026-07-13: structured chat defaults to Codex while explicitly opened raw terminals default to blank Shell. launchProfile and terminalLaunchProfile persist independently; terminal agent launches require an explicit profile choice; missing-command errors remain visible without overwriting the saved profile.

SPRODUCT-NAME-README · Product name and README docsQuality + shipping
v0_5

Lock the user-facing product name and give contributors a clear README/doc map without renaming the repo/package slug prematurely.

Done criteria

Product name is Keelhouse across PRD, README, product positioning, app metadata, generated roadmap, and docs index; repo/package/storage slug remains agent-cli until an explicit migration.

MAGENT-COMPOSER · Codex-style agent composerTerminal + process
v0_5

Historical terminal-composer slice. Superseded 2026-07-13: the interaction and app-action mechanics remain useful, but the product composer targets a structured chat rather than treating a terminal pane as the conversation.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08 for safe multiline PTY send, interrupt, draft/history keys, target context, and app-command routing. Those mechanics remain available in Raw terminal; DIRECT-AGENT-HARNESS owns the default structured-chat composer.

SSHORTCUTS · VS Code shortcut baselineChrome + interaction
v0_5

Keep VS Code muscle memory for the supported workflow: open folder, quick open/search, save, close, copy/paste, terminal focus, new pane, split/switch pane, and command palette.

Done criteria

A documented shortcut map covers the v0.5 workflow, matches VS Code defaults where applicable, appears in menu items/tooltips where relevant, and explicitly lists terminal/editor/app-chrome conflicts and exceptions.

MTERMINAL-ROBUSTNESS · Robust terminal parityTerminal + process
v0_5🔒

The terminal is the trusted agent surface, so it needs daily-driver robustness beyond the initial working loop.

Done criteria

Single-pane terminal verifies Claude/Codex TUIs, alternate screen apps, ANSI/truecolor styling, resize, scrollback, mouse selection, copy, paste, bracketed paste, common keyboard chords, command/cwd/status header, fast-output responsiveness, and screenshot/runtime QA.

MCONTEXT-MENUS · Right-click context menusChrome + interaction
v0_5

macOS pointer workflows need right-click/Control-click affordances, not only visible buttons. Context menus should expose the same command registry as menu bar, shortcuts, and command palette where possible.

Done criteria

Corrected 2026-07-13: one shared async, keyboard-accessible renderer covers workspace/project/chat, file, editor, browser, Git, composer, utility-tab, and terminal-pane surfaces. Close Project performs stop/remove/fallback lifecycle behavior; utility and pane tabs expose app-owned focus/restart/kill/close/hide actions; failures receive visible feedback.

MCHROME-POLISH-SYSTEM · Codex-level chrome polish systemChrome + interaction
v0_5

Define the workbench chrome system before more surfaces drift: dense rails, calm dark surfaces, tokens, menus, badges, pane headers, settings/sidebar rhythm, command palette, toasts, and restrained motion.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: current v0.5 chrome uses primitive -> semantic -> component CSS tokens, shared control/menu/badge/field/overlay states, visible focus, disabled/danger/success/warning states, reduced-motion handling, and screenshot QA for one-project workbench, context menu, recovery alerts, narrow layout, and a chrome state palette. Icon replacement remains ICON-SYSTEM; browser and diff/review now consume these tokens, while settings and command palette remain future surfaces.

MICON-SYSTEM · Codex-quality icon and status systemChrome + interaction
v0_5

The app should feel like a polished Codex-style workbench, not a raw prototype. Define one consistent icon language for project rail, file explorer, editor, browser, terminal, agent panes, git state, and common actions.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: current v0.5 chrome uses lucide-react through app/src/icons.tsx as one SVG icon contract. File-tree object icons, rail actions, editor actions, terminal status pills, composer controls, browser preview, source-control/diff entry points, context-menu rows, and QA fixtures use currentColor icons on a 16px dense grid. Non-universal controls keep labels, tooltips, or accessible names. Pane status and future activity status mappings cover thinking/running/waiting/error/exited/complete. Source-host and multi-pane-specific icons remain deferred until those real surfaces land.

SACCESSIBILITY-BASICS · Keyboard and focus accessibility baselineChrome + interaction
v0_5

A dense tool still needs visible focus, keyboard reachability, and labelled controls so it can be used without pointer precision.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: current v0.5 chrome has explicit labels for rail actions, workspace root, agent profile picker, terminal canvas, composer draft, browser preview controls, and diff review controls; editor tabs put tab semantics on the actual focusable buttons; the terminal canvas is tabbable and has a visible focus ring; context menus focus the first enabled item, support Arrow Up/Down, and close with Escape. Full modal trapping, canvas transcript screen-reader review, and deeper settings/multi-pane accessibility rules remain deferred.

MPROJECT-RAIL · Left project rail for open workspacesProjects + sessions
v1🔒

The core multi-project workflow: Project A, Project B, Project C live in one app instead of separate VS Code windows. Use a persistent left project/workspace rail, similar in spirit to Codex's sidebar, rather than relying only on top tabs.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: the left rail now has persisted openProjects separate from recents, shows at least three project rows in QA, marks active/running/exited/attention states, promotes opened folders into the rail, prunes missing paths, and exposes switch/reveal/copy/close context-menu actions. Switching projects uses the real workspace load path and restores in-session editor tabs, active file, editor buffers, and view state. Project session rows, browser preview URL state, and multiple live panes are now implemented; richer lifecycle state and cross-relaunch session restore stay with their dedicated later cards.

MPROJECT-SESSIONS · Project session rowsProjects + sessions
v1🔒

Historical workspace-context slice. Superseded 2026-07-13: project rows now represent real chats with independent messages/provider identity while retaining the saved editor/browser/raw-terminal context implemented here.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09 as workbench-session metadata: projectSessions and activeSessionByProject persist in workspace.json; the rail supports create, switch, rename, delete protection, and collapse; selection restores editor/browser context. Product semantics were corrected 2026-07-13 by DIRECT-AGENT-HARNESS; ProjectSession remains an internal compatibility name, not evidence that chats are terminal sessions.

MBROWSER-PREVIEW · Browser and web preview paneChrome + interaction
v1🔒

The stripped VS Code workbench still needs a browser surface for localhost apps, docs, auth flows, and agent-generated pages without context switching.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: the workbench has a docked iframe browser preview pane between editor and terminal with Back, Forward, Reload, address/local URL input, Open, and Open externally controls. Preview URLs are normalized, remembered per project and active project session in workspace.json, restored on project/session switch, and exposed through a browser context menu with copy/open/navigation actions. Dev-server auto-detection and agent-controlled browser actions remain with their dedicated later cards.

LPANE-MANAGER · Multiple agent panes per projectTerminal + process
v1🔒

Jason often runs multiple Claude/Codex sessions against the same project, and different open projects may run different agents at the same time. Add a pane manager that can create/switch/close multiple terminal panes per project.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: backend PtyState owns multiple real pty/process panes keyed by pane id; open_workspace spawns/focuses a pane without killing panes from other projects; create_pane, focus_pane, and close_pane add, focus, and safely kill exactly one selected pane. Grid events include paneId; the frontend caches snapshots by pane and paints only the focused pane. The terminal header exposes a focused pane strip plus New pane profile, New, and Close controls. Project/session state aggregates all panes for the project. Pane names, restart/kill-all, layout restore, and app-owned handles remain with their dedicated later cards.

SPANE-NAMES · Pane names and task labelsTerminal + process
v1

When several agents run in one project, each pane needs a human label so Jason can remember what it is doing.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: terminal panes have editable task labels shown in the pane strip and composer target. Labels normalize input, fall back to profile/index names when blank, persist in workspace.json by project-session plus pane slot, and can be edited by double-clicking the pane pill or using Rename Selected Pane in the terminal context menu.

MAGENT-SESSION-HANDLE · App-owned agent session handlesTerminal + process
v1🔒

Define the missing middle layer between real pty panes and the Codex-style composer: a stable app-owned handle for each pane/session.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: app/src/agentSessionHandle.ts defines stable pane-backed handles with project/session ids, cwd, pane label, profile id/label, process state, approval mode, exit code, created time, activity metadata, and send/interrupt/readTail/close operations. The composer now sends prompts through the active handle; Stop and Close route through handle methods; terminal context menu can copy the latest 20 rendered terminal lines through readTail. Activity log and future hooks can target the handle contract instead of UI component state.

MAGENT-ACTIVITY · Agent activity and thinking statesTerminal + process
v1

Background agents need Codex-style visible status and activity rows when they think, run commands, edit files, call tools, wait for approval/input, error, exit, or complete. Show concise activity/progress summaries and tool events, not hidden chain-of-thought.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: app/src/agentActivity.ts normalizes in-memory activity rows scoped by project/session/pane. The selected pane shows a current status row from its handle plus recent rows for prompt sent, app command ran/failed, edited file, created pane, stop sent, copied output, pane closed, complete, and command failed on non-zero exit. Rows render above the composer with shared icons/status colors and accessible labels; the QA fixture covers running, prompt, edited file, ran command, and failed command rows. Approval rows and durable/filterable history are covered by APP-ACTIONS-MINIMAL and AGENT-ACTIVITY-LOG.

MAGENT-ACTIVITY-LOG · Agent activity timelineTerminal + process
v1

Each pane/session needs an inspectable timeline of user-safe agent events so Jason can see what happened without reading every terminal line.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: agentActivityEvents now persists up to 200 user-safe events with project/session/pane attribution, kind, label, detail, status, timestamp, target, exit code, output reference, and undo hint fields. The selected pane shows a durable timeline below the activity strip with filters for prompts, process, commands, files, tools, git, app actions, approvals, browser, errors, and completion. Startup normalization drops malformed stored rows. Current producers cover prompts, app commands, app-action approvals including Open Diff, file saves, pane lifecycle actions, copied output, and process exits; git mutation, richer browser, and tool producers populate the reserved event fields when those real surfaces land.

MAPP-ACTIONS-MINIMAL · Minimal app-owned action gateTerminal + process
v1

Permission controls need real enforcement behind them. Add the small action gate for app-owned actions before showing Codex-style approval modes.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: app/src/appActions.ts defines app-owned action descriptors with kind, target, risk class, requester, reason, and undo hint; resolves each action to an audit event with approval mode, decision, prompt state, timestamp, and reason. Existing app-owned paths for focus pane, open file, open diff, open browser preview, attach reference, interrupt process, create pane, close pane, and composer commands route through the gate. Prompted, denied, blocked, and composer-originated decisions log approval audit rows to the activity timeline.

MCOMPOSER-HARNESS · Composer harness controlsTerminal + process
v1

Use the bottom composer as the real Codex-style control surface for each structured chat: attachments, approval mode, goal, model override, reasoning effort, context, and mutually exclusive stop/send behavior.

Done criteria

Corrected 2026-07-13: composer state is scoped per project/chat in composerHarnessBySession. The visible footer exposes functional attachment, Ask/Approve safe/Full access, goal, Codex model override, reasoning effort, and one Send position that becomes Stop during an active run. Goal and attachments enter prompt context; model and reasoning overrides reach both new and resumed codex exec runs; invalid native overrides are rejected. Unsupported voice input is not rendered as a dead control.

MPROCESS-LIFECYCLE · Agent process lifecycle controlsTerminal + process
v1

A cockpit needs basic process affordances: restart, kill, status, exited state, and command/cwd visibility.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: backend terminate_pane kills a pane child process while keeping the pane/transcript for exit state; backend restart_pane spawns and focuses a fresh process, then removes/kills the replaced process. The frontend preserves pane slot and label across restart, updates project/session status, clears stale snapshots, logs terminate/restart events, and routes both actions through the action gate. The terminal header shows command, mode, cwd, state, Restart, Kill, New, and Close, with narrow responsive icon controls.

MAGENT-FIRST-WORKBENCH · Agent-first resizable tray workbenchChrome + interaction
v1

Historical tray-hierarchy slice. Superseded 2026-07-13: structured chat and composer own the main screen; raw terminal, editor, files, Git, and browser remain the switchable/resizable surfaces proven here.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-10 for movable tool/tray behavior and raw-terminal fidelity. The terminal-backed Run assumption was rejected in native QA and replaced by DIRECT-AGENT-HARNESS; only the tray/docking work remains current product guidance.

MVSCODE-CHROME-SHELL · Keelhouse shell chromeChrome + interaction
v1🔒

Correct the outer application chrome toward the useful VS Code/Codex structure without copying controls that do not fit Keelhouse.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: the app shell now uses titlebar/sidebar/main/status grid geometry. The header shows real Keelhouse state and actions: workspace, session, selected agent/pane, process status, Open Folder, and Reload Preview. Fake traffic lights, fake back/forward, the search-shaped folder picker, and the nonfunctional activity rail were removed. Activity drawer navigation remains parked until it switches real drawer content.

SDRAWER-MODES-PROJECTS-FILES · Projects and Files drawer modesChrome + interaction
v1

Split the side drawer into real modes instead of stacking project/session rows above the file tree.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: the side drawer has Projects and Files tabs. Projects shows open projects and task sessions; Files shows workspace root, file actions, and the virtualized tree. The Files drawer reattaches its height observer when opened. Screenshot QA defaults to Projects mode. Verified with npm run build, npm test, npm run qa:editor, selected screenshot inspection, and git diff --check.

MACTIVITY-DRAWERS · Switchable activity drawersChrome + interaction
v1🔒

Continue drawer navigation only when it is functional, not decorative. Borrow Zed's agentic layout principle: project/session thread sidebar plus rearrangeable real panels, not a decorative VS Code rail clone. Remaining useful drawers are Search, Source Control/Git, Browser/tools, and Settings.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: the side drawer has Projects, Files, Search, Git, Browser, and Settings modes. Search filters the live file tree and opens editor matches; Git reads real git status through Rust; Browser controls the preview URL/history/reload/external open actions; Settings changes current profile, permission mode, agent surface, tray position, open folder, and file refresh. Drawer width is pointer/keyboard resizable; collapsed icon-only state and width persist locally. Verified with npm run build, npm test, cargo test, npm run qa:editor, selected screenshot inspection, and git diff --check.

MSESSION-RESTORE · Restore projects, files, panesProjects + sessions
v1

After quit/relaunch, the cockpit should come back as the same working context, without pretending to preserve live agent process state.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: sessionEditorSnapshots persists per-session editor tabs, active file, dirty buffers, and CodeMirror view state. paneLayoutsBySession persists pane slots, launch profile ids, and labels. Startup normalizes both stores before reopening the last workspace and creates fresh panes from the saved layout without pretending live process memory or transcripts survived relaunch. Covered by npm run build and npm test -- sessionRestore.test.ts.

SGIT-STATUS · Git status in file railFiles + editor
v1

Small but high-signal replacement for a VS Code affordance Jason likely relies on: dirty/new/deleted markers in the file tree.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: file nodes show compact Git tokens for modified, untracked/new, added, deleted, renamed, and staged files. Deleted files that no longer exist on disk are inserted as virtual file rows under their parent folder and cannot be opened as real files. Unsaved editor draft dots remain separate from repository status. Covered by npm run build, npm test -- fileGitStatus.test.ts, and npm run qa:editor.

MDIFF-VIEW · Diff view for agent changesFiles + editor
v1

Jason supervises agents by reading what changed. A file status marker is not enough; he needs to inspect diffs without opening VS Code.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: Source Control rows and file-rail Git context menus open a read-only unified diff review in the editor tray. The review shows source/status, additions/deletions, old/new line numbers, diff metadata, synthetic untracked text diffs, and hunk-to-editor jumps for files that still exist. Covered by npm run build, npm test -- diffView.test.ts, cargo test git_, and cargo test synthesizes_untracked_text_diff.

SGIT-ACTIONS-LITE · Lightweight git review actionsFiles + editor
v1🔒

Add only the git actions needed to review/clean agent work, not a full git client.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: diff review and Git-marked file context menus expose Stage, Unstage, Discard Unstaged Changes, and Copy shown diff. The blind audit findings were folded into the card: discard is blocked when the same file has an unsaved editor draft; mixed staged/unstaged files use explicit labels (Copy shown diff, whole-file Stage/Unstage); backend git_file_action validates repository-relative paths and re-reads target status before mutation; staged new files, tracked discard, and untracked discard use separate Git command paths; real temp-repo Git tests cover stage/unstage/discard; the slice stops at per-file actions, not branch/commit/push/PR; screenshot QA includes diff-review.png. Covered by npm run build, npm test, cargo test git_file_actions, npm run qa:editor, and git diff --check.

SCHROME-DEMO-CONTRACT · Accepted chrome demo contractChrome + interaction
v1🔒

Treat demo/keelhouse-chrome-demo.html as the visual contract for the app pass.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: the accepted demo contract is documented in docs/chrome-contract.md, enforced by npm run qa:chrome-contract, and tied to accepted demo plus real app shell checks.

SCHROME-QA-GATE · Chrome screenshot QA gateQuality + shipping
v1

Every chrome pass must prove the accepted states still hold visually and mechanically.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-10: npm run qa:shell captures the actual React shell at 1440x900, 1024x640, and 900x640; npm run qa:chrome-contract requires those artifacts plus the accepted token/control/run-surface contract. The deterministic editor fixture remains for menu, diff, modal, and failure states but is no longer accepted as sole app-shell proof. Native Tauri proof is stored at docs/qa/app-shell/native-run.png.

SSTEEL-CYAN-THEME-GATE · Graphite and steel-cyan theme gateChrome + interaction
v1

Lock the accepted graphite shell and steel-cyan accent into semantic tokens and QA checks.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-09: app chrome defines steel-cyan primitive tokens, routes semantic accent tokens through them, removes the old blue primary treatment, flattens shared badge/control radii, and the chrome contract gate rejects warm accent drift.

MAGENT-THREAD-SURFACE · Historical terminal-backed run surfaceChrome + interaction
v1🔒

Historical implementation slice. Superseded 2026-07-13: the exact raw-terminal renderer remains useful, but structured chat rather than a terminal viewport is the default working surface.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-10 for readable PTY output and exact raw-terminal switching. The default terminal-backed Run plus separate Activity model was rejected in native QA and replaced by DIRECT-AGENT-HARNESS. This card no longer defines the center surface.

MAPP-CHROME-PORT · Port accepted chrome into appChrome + interaction
v1🔒

Port the accepted demo into the real Tauri app shell without losing current v1 functionality.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-10 for native shell integration and existing file/editor/browser/Git/terminal paths. Its terminal-backed center was superseded 2026-07-13 by the continuous structured chat shell; the implementation and screenshots remain historical evidence, not the current visual contract.

SCHROME-CONTROL-POLISH · Native-feeling controls and iconsChrome + interaction
v1

Flatten obvious AI-looking rounded rectangles into native-feeling toolbar, menu, dropdown, and command controls.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-10: redundant recent-activity chips and nine inline tray/dock buttons were removed. Drawer modes are icon tabs; run settings and tool/dock choices use compact labelled popovers; toolbar controls stay flat with shared outline icons, focus/hover/disabled states, and labels only for ambiguous commands. Actual-shell and native screenshots confirm the reduced chrome.

MTRAY-DOCKING-UX · Movable resizable tool traysChrome + interaction
v1

Editor and browser are optional resizable tools around the agent run, not a permanent stacked workbench.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-10: first open hides the tool tray, opening Tools defaults to Editor, and one compact menu exposes Editor, Browser, Split, left, right, bottom, and hidden states. Layout and sizes persist; narrow side docks become bottom docks; hidden-tray CSS is regression-tested at 900px; single-tool modes remove the unused surface and splitter.

MCHROME-COHERENCE-PASS · Workbench chrome coherence passChrome + interaction
v1🔒

Fix the actual shell before measurement using VS Code/Zed-level chrome craft without copying either product, with demo/cockpit-demo.html as the graphite mood reference and Codex as the clean-composition reference: one large agent-first window, a direct thread/project sidebar on the left, no decorative left activity rail, flat toolbar icons, steel-cyan accent, minimal rounded button chrome, resizable splitters, and editor/browser/git/raw terminal as docked tool panes only when needed. Target walkthrough: demo/keelhouse-chrome-demo.html.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-10: the actual first-open shell reads as one agent-first cockpit at 1440, 1024, and the 900px minimum. Projects/sessions remain a direct left drawer; Run owns the center; editor/browser are hidden until requested; compact menus replace button clusters; splitters and responsive geometry are typed and tested. Deterministic fixtures retain menu/settings/palette/error coverage, while actual React and native Tauri screenshots prove the shell and live PTY path.

SDEV-SERVER-DETECT · Local dev server detectionChrome + interaction
v1

When Claude/Codex starts Vite, Next, Astro, or another dev server, the app should make the local preview obvious without acting like a full browser. Current slice detects local URLs from terminal output and offers an explicit browser-preview action.

Done criteria

Detected localhost URLs from terminal output can be opened in the browser preview; the preview remembers the latest URL per project/session and offers reload/open-external. OS-level port discovery remains deferred until daily-driver metrics show it is needed. Executed 2026-07-11: detection, gated open, per-project/session preview persistence, and reload/open-external all shipped and test-covered (browserPreview.test.ts); OS-level port discovery stays deferred per criteria.

SCOMMAND-PALETTE · VS Code-style command paletteChrome + interaction
v2

A compact VS Code-style way to expose actions without adding IDE chrome. Current slice adds a real Shift+Cmd+P palette for existing app-owned actions: open folder, save/find/close tab, pane create/restart/kill/close/clear, browser reload/open detected, drawer switches, tray switches, and composer context attachments.

Done criteria

A keyboard palette can run core app actions, shows shortcuts where assigned, and stays consistent with menu/context-menu labels. Current slice keeps Cmd+P quick-open, ripgrep text search, and terminal scrollback search with SEARCH and TERMINAL-FIND. Executed 2026-07-11: palette runs core app actions with shortcut labels consistent with menus/context menus; demo overlay parity applied; quick-open/text-search live under SEARCH.

MSEARCH · Find files and textFiles + editor
v2

A lightweight replacement for the VS Code search/file-jump behaviors that matter while supervising agents. Current slice adds Cmd+P Quick Open and Files/Text scopes in the Search drawer.

Done criteria

Keyboard-driven file quick-open and ripgrep-backed text search work inside the selected project. Current text search uses rg --json when available, falls back to the ignore-aware walker, caps file size/results, and opens hits at the matching line. Executed 2026-07-11: Cmd+P quick-open and rg-backed text search verified (workspaceSearch.test.ts, cargo workspace_text_search). Cross-project/session search deferred: no observed daily-driver need yet (anti-drift), revisit after DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE.

SDAILY-DRIVER-METRICS · Daily-driver success metricsQuality + shipping
v1

Readiness gate for the three target replacement workflows (one-project edit+agent+preview, two-agent same-project, three-project switch/relaunch): verifies the required surfaces, docs, and screenshot evidence exist and writes docs/qa/daily-driver reports. Blind-audit 2026-07-11 clarification: this card proves readiness only — timed live runs, VS Code comparison, and the North-Star claim belong to DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE and must not be cited as proven from this gate.

Done criteria

Script and record three workflow runs: one-project edit+agent, two-agent same-project, and three-project switch/relaunch. Current readiness gate verifies required app surfaces, docs, and screenshots exist and writes docs/qa/daily-driver/latest.json plus latest.md; timed live Tauri runs, elapsed time, crashes, memory/CPU snapshots, and VS Code-needed checks remain next. Executed 2026-07-11 as the readiness gate (qa:daily-driver green, evidence at docs/qa/daily-driver/); timed live runs and VS Code comparison are DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE scope, not this card.

MTERMINAL-FIND · Find in terminal scrollbackTerminal + process
v2

Agent output can be long. Search inside terminal scrollback is necessary for reviewing logs and prior agent output.

Done criteria

Find text in the active pane scrollback, jump next/previous, and preserve terminal focus behavior. Executed 2026-07-11: backend Screen-space search + viewport jump with real-VT Rust test, find strip UI with prev/next/count/preview, palette command; docs/terminal-robustness.md records scope and deferred regex/cell-highlighting.

MSETTINGS · Settings for commands, themes, and shortcutsChrome + interaction
v2

Keep settings boring and inspectable: agent commands, AI connections, source-control connections, ignored folders, font, color theme, default layout, browser preview, hooks, git/worktree defaults, environments, and shortcut overrides. This is not a plugin platform.

Done criteria

A local config file controls agent profiles, AI connections, source-control connections, font/theme, ignored folders, default pane layout, browser preview, hooks, Git/worktree behavior, environments, and app-level keybindings; bad config reports actionable errors and plugins/extensions are not supported. Executed 2026-07-11: settings modal now covers agent commands (launch profiles), permission default, layout, browser preview URL, git health, ignored-folders (read-only mirror of the backend filter), theme/font status, and a read-only active-shortcut reference table from shortcuts.ts. AI connections stay with AI-CONNECTIONS; shortcut overrides stay with KEYBINDINGS-CONFIG; theme variants stay with THEME.

SKEYBINDINGS-CONFIG · Configurable keybindingsChrome + interaction
v2

After the VS Code-compatible default shortcut map stabilizes, allow focused overrides without hardcoding personal preferences into the app.

Done criteria

A local config file can override app-level shortcuts, reports conflicts with terminal/editor reserved bindings, and generates a visible shortcut reference. Executed 2026-07-11: keybindingOverrides persist in workspace.json (normalized on load, active shortcuts only), chrome-scope key handling resolves through comboMatches/resolveShortcutKeys so overrides apply live and labels update everywhere via shortcutKeys, conflict detection warns inline in the settings Keyboard shortcuts category, per-row Rebind capture (single combo, Escape cancels) + Reset for active shortcuts; native-menu accelerators and CodeMirror keymaps stay registry-bound (documented boundary). 136 vitest incl. override/conflict/combo tests.

STHEME · Color themesChrome + interaction
v2

Support practical color themes across chrome, terminal, rail, and editor. Start with the approved mono-ghost theme; do not expand into plugin theming or break the chrome token contract.

Done criteria

App chrome, terminal canvas, file rail, and editor can switch between built-in color themes with readable contrast while preserving semantic token roles and component states. Executed 2026-07-11: mono-ghost theme as a token-only [data-theme] remap (component contract preserved; recurring hexes promoted to tokens first), persisted appTheme setting with selector in Settings, contract gate green with graphite as reference, mono-ghost verified in the running shell.

STRANSCRIPTS · Pane transcript captureTerminal + process
v2

Completed agent sessions should be reviewable after the process exits without keeping panes open forever.

Done criteria

A pane can save a plain-text transcript/log path for later review, with clear retention/reset behavior. Executed 2026-07-12: pane transcripts captured on exit and via a Save Transcript context action, persisted per project/session in paneTranscripts (capped 60 / 100k chars, empty dropped, tail-truncated), reviewable in a read-only modal (Review Transcripts palette command) listing by pane label + relative time. Pure helpers tested (145 vitest). Text-only, no ANSI replay.

SNOTIFICATIONS · Background agent notificationsChrome + interaction
v2

When an agent in another project or pane stops or needs input, Jason should not have to manually inspect every pane.

Done criteria

Background panes can surface in-app badges and optional macOS notifications for exit/attention events. Executed 2026-07-12: background-exit badge count on project-rail rows (backgroundExits.ts pure helpers + tests), cleared on project focus; optional native macOS notification via tauri-plugin-notification gated behind a default-off Settings toggle that only requests permission when enabled. 141 vitest / 36 cargo.

MWORKTREE · Worktree helperTerminal + process
v2

One keystroke: new pane = git worktree add + agent launched inside it, named and disposable. The 'multiple agents on one repo, parallel' workflow.

Done criteria

New Worktree Pane / Remove Worktree wired end-to-end: Rust create_project_worktree/remove_project_worktree (git worktree add/remove --force + branch -D), App.tsx createWorktreePane/closeWorktreePane, worktrees.ts store helpers persisted to workspace.json. Verified: cargo test 39/39 (incl. a real-repo worktree add/remove round-trip test), npm test 152/152 (incl. worktrees.test.ts), npm run build, cargo fmt --check, qa:chrome-contract all green. Gap: no live `npm run tauri dev` smoke test creating/removing a real worktree pane through the UI yet — build-verified, not native-UI-verified.

SINTEGRATIONS-POLICY · Integration adapter policyQuality + shipping
v2

Define the allowed lane for other integrations so Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, Linear, Jira, Slack, or Discord do not turn the app into a plugin hub.

Done criteria

Documented integration policy classifies integrations as core, first-class, adapter-lane, parked, or out-of-scope; every integration requires a concrete workflow, health check, credential boundary, and app-owned command surface. Executed 2026-07-11: policy section added to docs/integrations-scope.md with the five lanes, four per-integration requirements, parked/out-of-scope lists, and promotion paths.

SSESSION-ARCHIVE · Session archiveProjects + sessions
v2

Hide completed project chats without losing their messages, provider thread ids, transcript references, or workspace context.

Done criteria

A completed project chat can be archived/restored and stays out of the main rail while retaining messages, provider identity, transcript references, and metadata. Executed 2026-07-12 as session metadata; 2026-07-13 chat-model correction requires the same archive flag to preserve chatConversations records.

SREUSE-AUDIT · Mine old projects for reusable piecesResearch & spikes
v3

Re-check Hallmark/hashmark/brutal/indx for reusable Tauri shell, persistence, editor, or design patterns without adopting their product shape.

Done criteria

Reusable components/patterns are listed with exact source paths and either imported, adapted, or explicitly rejected. Amended 2026-07-13 after the original repo-only audit missed Hashmark: docs/reuse-audit.md now maps Hashmark's SQLite sessions/messages, provider event streams, approvals, composer context, session forks/checkpoints, MCP/OAuth, and orchestration into dependency-ordered Keelhouse cards; it rejects Hashmark's editor/orchestrator chrome and records unsafe implementation details that must not be copied.

MCHROME-CONTROL-GRAMMAR · Demo control grammar: flat actionsChrome + interaction
v1🔒

Replace the boxed default-button chrome with the accepted demo's three-control grammar: flat text actions (weight 600, hover to accent-strong), transparent icon buttons (color-only hover), and a single filled Send. Danger actions (Kill, Stop, Discard) become flat text-actions in danger color; confirmations keep existing modals. Hit areas stay at least 40px via padding even where glyphs are 16px.

Done criteria

No default-variant control in titlebar, terminal toolbar, editor actions, browser toolbar, composer, or drawer renders with the boxed control background and border. Only Send (filled accent) and modal-confirm primaries are filled. 1440/1024/900 qa:shell screenshots show flat chrome; focus rings and 40px hit areas verified; npm test and npm run build pass. Executed 2026-07-11: native live-pty proof at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png (real Tauri window, live shell PTY in the centered Run card, populated activity, real recency stamps, persisted layout honored).

MRUN-SURFACE-COMPOSITION · Run surface demo compositionChrome + interaction
v1

Compose the center as one continuous structured chat with inline provider/app events and an anchored composer. Keep chat visible while Terminal/Processes/Logs open in the demo's collapsible, resizable bottom utility tray; Browser remains in the right dock and provider structure is never inferred from terminal text.

Done criteria

Corrected and native-verified 2026-07-13: removed the separate Run/Activity center and the alternate-center raw-terminal mode. Structured chat remains primary; a real Codex PTY opens in the bottom tray, Processes shows live panes, and collapse returns to the 42px tab strip without changing chat.

SCOMPOSER-ELEVATION · Elevated demo composer cardChrome + interaction
v1

Rebuild the composer as the demo's elevated card: 12px radius, 1px #343642 border, deep soft shadow, gradient fade wrap, borderless textarea with 78px minimum, 34px bottom bar with flat chips for permission mode, goal, profile, and attachments, and a 24px filled up-arrow Send; Stop becomes a flat danger text-action.

Done criteria

Composer matches the demo spec in qa:shell screenshots; Enter/Shift+Enter/Escape/Up/Down, history, app-commands, and attachment behavior regressions all pass in npm test; the 900px narrow layout keeps chips from wrapping into overlap. Executed 2026-07-11: native live-pty proof at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png (real Tauri window, live shell PTY in the centered Run card, populated activity, real recency stamps, persisted layout honored).

SSIDEBAR-RHYTHM · Thread sidebar demo rhythmChrome + interaction
v1

Apply the demo thread-sidebar spec: 11px/600 uppercase section labels in faint ink, 34px minimum rows, project rows at 600 weight with 7px status dots, session rows indented 28px with real recency stamps (now/4h/1d from session last-activity), left-stripe actives, and quiet hover. Row-level pointer activation replaces boxed Switch affordances; context menus keep the full action set.

Done criteria

Projects drawer matches the demo sidebar spec in screenshots; recency stamps render real relative times; switch/rename/close flows still pass their tests. Executed 2026-07-11: native live-pty proof at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png (real Tauri window, live shell PTY in the centered Run card, populated activity, real recency stamps, persisted layout honored).

STITLEBAR-STATUSBAR-PARITY · Titlebar and statusbar parityChrome + interaction
v1

36px titlebar with the demo gradient, centered workspace crumb (strong project name plus faint branch from real git status), flat right action cluster with an accent-strong agent chip; 24px statusbar with three 12px sections: branch/sync/errors, agent status/panes, and shortcut hints.

Done criteria

Titlebar and statusbar match the spec at all three QA widths; all existing titlebar actions remain reachable as flat labelled controls with 40px hit areas; the drag region still works in the native window. Executed 2026-07-11: native live-pty proof at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png (real Tauri window, live shell PTY in the centered Run card, populated activity, real recency stamps, persisted layout honored).

MTRAY-TAB-CHROME · Tabbed tray chrome, movable docksChrome + interaction
v1

Keep useWorkbenchLayout docking (left/right/bottom, resizable, persisted) while the dock renders the approved Files, Editor, Browser, and Git tab strip with steel-cyan underline actives. Files and Git are real tray surfaces, not duplicate left-drawer modes; the left column remains project threads.

Done criteria

Corrected 2026-07-13: Files/Editor/Browser/Git are functional right-dock tabs. Terminal/Processes/Logs are functional bottom-tray tabs; Browser Preview duplication was removed. Utility and terminal-pane tabs have app-owned context menus, and pane selection uses a flat underline rather than rounded capsule chrome.

SFIRST-OPEN-LAYOUT · First open shows demo layoutChrome + interaction
v1

First-open default is the demo layout: Threads drawer open, centered conversation and composer, Files-first right dock, collapsed bottom utility tray, and statusbar. Returning users keep persisted layouts; Reset restores the demo geometry and collapsed tray.

Done criteria

Corrected and native-verified 2026-07-13: the overlay-titlebar package opens with the approved Threads/chat/right-dock/bottom-tray hierarchy. Reset restores sidebar, chat, Files-right, sizing, and collapsed utilities. Opening Terminal no longer replaces the conversation.

SOVERLAY-PARITY · Menus and palette demo parityChrome + interaction
v1

Align existing overlays to the demo spec. Context menus: compact icon/label/shortcut rows and quiet hover. Command palette: dense input and result rows with background-only active state. Settings navigation also uses background-only selection. Overlay scrim keeps restrained blur.

Done criteria

Menus and palette match the spec in screenshots; keyboard nav, Escape, and focus behavior regressions pass; the settings modal spec is documented in docs/settings-parity.md for the v2 card. Executed 2026-07-11: menus/palette aligned and screenshot-verified, keyboard regressions green, settings modal spec recorded in docs/settings-parity.md.

LDIRECT-AGENT-HARNESS · Codex-style multi-chat harnessTerminal + process
v1

Primary product correction from native QA: project rows contain multiple independent Codex-style chats, not terminal sessions wearing chat chrome. Persist user/assistant/tool messages and provider thread ids; render structured provider events; keep raw PTYs as an optional alternate view and adapter fallback.

Progress

Executed 2026-07-13 in the rebuilt packaged app: two same-project Codex chats produced independent structured histories, resumed their own provider threads, and restored after quit/relaunch. A post-relaunch context question recovered the pre-relaunch token without restating it. Both chats ran shell-backed turns concurrently; Stop initially exposed wrapper-only cancellation, then chat_harness was fixed to isolate and terminate the selected run's full process group. The rebuilt app returned Chat A to Ready within 1.8s while Chat B stayed Running and completed CHAT-B-SURVIVED. Raw terminal replaced the center and toggled back without entering chat history. 178 frontend tests and 47 Rust tests pass. Evidence: docs/qa/daily-driver/codex-multi-chat.md and codex-multi-chat-native.png.

Done criteria

The packaged app creates two same-project chats, receives structured Codex output in both through existing OAuth, switches without mixed messages, resumes each provider thread for a second turn, stops a live run, restores chat histories after relaunch, and never renders raw terminal text as a chat message. Gemini/Claude adapter gaps are explicit and route to raw terminal.

MCHAT-DURABLE-STORE · Durable SQLite chat storeTerminal + process
v1🔒

Adapt Hashmark's SQLite WAL session/message model so real chat history is not capped inside workspace.json. Keep lightweight layout preferences in Tauri Store; persist chats, provider thread ids, message blocks, run status, usage, and schema migrations in SQLite.

Done criteria

Executed 2026-07-13: two workspace.json chats and 29 messages migrated once without loss; transaction rollback and stale-revision tests protect message blocks; provider thread ids, 32 post-run messages, and usage restored after a packaged Codex quit/relaunch; interrupted runs recover explicitly; malformed usage cannot crash startup; tests cover migration, cascade delete, concurrent chats, interrupted writes, and provider item-id reuse.

MCHAT-RICH-MESSAGES · Codex-grade message renderingChrome + interaction
v1🔒

Completed 2026-07-13: safe Markdown/GFM, highlighted code copy, progressive provider deltas, compact tool rows, retry/error recovery, elapsed state, scroll anchoring, Jump to latest, and accessible run status render in the structured chat surface without raw HTML, avatars, hidden reasoning, or terminal-text inference.

Progress

Packaged native inspection rendered a preserved real Markdown turn with heading, list, safe link, fenced TypeScript highlighting, language label, and working Code copied state. The persisted multi-chat transcript exposes the previously executed process-group Stop as Codex run stopped while the cancelled completion token remains absent. Automated coverage verifies valid GFM tables, unsafe HTML/link/image rejection, progressive updates, compact tool states, retry ownership, scroll anchoring, Jump to latest, elapsed labels, and live-region semantics.

Done criteria

Executed 2026-07-13: current packaged app visual inspection plus the real Codex stop/recovery transcript satisfy the native rendering and Stop criteria; 218 frontend tests, 62 Rust tests, production build, and diff check pass. Evidence: docs/qa/chat-rich-messages/README.md and docs/qa/daily-driver/codex-multi-chat.md.

MCHAT-PROVIDER-APPROVALS · Real provider tool approvalsTerminal + process
v1

Adapt Hashmark's pending-approval registry and inline Allow/Deny pattern at the provider boundary. The existing sandbox selector is not enough: consequential Codex tool requests must pause with exact command/path/diff context and resume only the selected chat run.

Progress

Completed 2026-07-13. Run-scoped app-server requests render exact action/cwd/target/reason and resolve through Deny, Allow once, or Allow for session. Decisions persist with run id, resolution source, and timestamp. Timeout and run close cancel pending requests. Packaged native QA executed allow, deny, stop, concurrent-chat completion, and relaunch restoration; direct provider allow/cancel smokes also passed. Evidence: docs/qa/chat-provider-approvals/README.md.

Done criteria

A real Codex tool request can pause one chat, show exact action/risk/target, Allow once, Deny with feedback, or grant a narrowly scoped session permission; another chat keeps running; timeout/close resolves denied; decisions are persisted and attributed; Full access remains an explicit mode rather than the default; native QA executes allow and deny paths.

MCHAT-HISTORY-DISCOVERY · Chat search, pin, and bookmarksProjects + sessions
v1

Completed 2026-07-13: global chat discovery searches titles and SQLite message text across projects, opens the exact matched message, filters persistent bookmarks, sorts pinned chats within project groups, and reopens archived results without losing provider identity.

Done criteria

Executed 2026-07-13 in the packaged app: searched CHAT-A-ONE across projects, opened the exact result, bookmarked a message, pinned and archived New chat 6, found and reopened the archived chat, and confirmed bookmark/archive/pin persistence after relaunch. The pre-QA user state was restored afterward. Rust migration, cross-project search, bookmark persistence, 3,000-message latency, and frontend wiring tests pass. Evidence: docs/chat-history-discovery.md.

MPACKAGING · Local app packagingQuality + shipping
v1

Make it launch like a real macOS app, not only from `npm run tauri dev`.

Progress

Complete. Distribution notarization and Developer ID signing remain separate release/distribution work; local packaging is no longer blocked.

Done criteria

Executed 2026-07-12: `npm run package:mac` produces an arm64, ad-hoc-signed Keelhouse.app. The scoped build script patches an ignored copy of Zig 0.15.2's libc++ without changing the installed toolchain. The packaged app launched, restored the real `agent cli` workspace, spawned a shell, and launched the installed Codex CLI as a child process. Setup and output paths are documented in docs/packaging.md.

LSESSION-PANE-ISOLATION · Isolate live panes by project threadTerminal + process
v1

Live packaged testing 2026-07-12 disproved the readiness assumption that pane layouts are session-scoped: creating New session 3 changed the thread label but retained the prior thread's five live panes. Key running panes by project + session, preserve each thread's active pane and layout, and switch same-project threads without sharing or relabeling another thread's processes.

Progress

Completed 2026-07-13. Live/active pane maps are keyed by project + session; switching and new-thread creation use the target context; project status aggregates contexts; background exits update the owning session; deletion closes only owned panes. Packaged QA ran Alpha and Beta shells in two same-project chats with independent labels, output, and process ids (59144 and 62755), switched without restart, then relaunched with fresh independent processes (84110 and 89143) and restored layouts. Evidence: docs/qa/daily-driver/session-pane-isolation.md.

Done criteria

A new thread starts its own default pane set; two same-project threads can run different agents concurrently; switching threads focuses only that thread's panes without restarting the other thread; relaunch restores ownership and layouts; unit tests cover keying/migration; a packaged live run records independent pane IDs and output for both threads.

LSETTINGS-PARITY · Scoped settings workspace parityChrome + interaction
v1

Finish the dedicated searchable settings workspace with truthful Global, Project, and Chat ownership, inherited overrides, provider profiles, command sources, and policy surfaces. Render only capabilities Keelhouse actually supports.

Progress

Completed and packaged 2026-07-13: Settings is a dedicated full-window destination with Personal/Workbench/Integrations navigation, search, Back/Escape return, and responsive category fallback. Chat provider, permission mode, and browser URL resolve Global -> Project -> Chat with inherited-source and Reset override controls. Provider health reports local Codex/Gemini/Claude CLI version, auth state, and structured/raw capability without returning credential output. Custom raw-terminal label/command profiles persist and appear in terminal menus; unsupported chat providers remain visible but unavailable instead of silently routing to Codex. Commands, Files, Open tabs, and Worktrees command-palette sources persist and filter live results. Project-owned worktree, lifecycle-hook, and environment rows state current behavior and unavailable execution honestly. Packaged QA exercised scope reset, provider/profile management, compact navigation, Files source off/on behavior, real README discovery, and all policy rows. Evidence: docs/settings-parity.md and docs/qa/settings-workspace/. Automated proof: 241 tests, production build, chrome contract, and macOS package.

Done criteria

Cmd+, and Settings open a dedicated full-height workspace with Back to app, search, stable grouped navigation, and responsive content. Applicable agent profiles, commands, worktree policy, hooks, environments, and connection values show their Global -> Project -> Chat scope and inheritance; no synthetic workspace-group scope appears. Keyboard shortcuts are searchable/rebindable; command-palette sources are configurable. Existing General, Appearance, Layout, Browser, Git, notifications, reset, and shortcut behavior remains functional. Unsupported account, billing, marketplace, and novelty categories stay absent. Native screenshots at desktop and narrow widths plus interaction tests prove navigation, scope changes, search, and return-to-workbench behavior.

SRUN-CONTENT-DENSITY-QA · Populated-state visual proofQuality + shipping
v1

Blind-audit 2026-07-11: every chrome screenshot proves the empty first-open state only. Capture the surfaces that carry the product: live PTY agent output flowing through the centered run card (including the native tauri-dev smoke at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png), five-plus stacked activity rows with long real labels, a populated three-project sidebar with sessions and recency stamps, and a multi-pane strip, at 1440/1024/900. Enforce a legibility floor: the run column may never compress below 600px or scroll horizontally with drawer plus docked tray open.

Done criteria

Populated screenshots checked into docs/qa/chrome-delta/ for run-with-live-output, stacked activity, three-project sidebar, and multi-pane states at all three widths; native live-pty capture exists; a test asserts the run column legibility floor; visual inspection confirms the demo composition survives real terminal density. Executed 2026-07-12: populated three-project sidebar (real session recency, real project switching, crash-recovery banner) captured at 1440/1024/900 in docs/qa/chrome-delta/density-populated-*.png; fluid run column confirmed legible at 900px with no compression. Gap: the stacked-activity-row density check was not captured — synthetic activity events for a pane the backend never spawned did not render (correctly; the app only surfaces real event provenance), and fabricating that scenario would mean mock evidence, not real proof. Real activity-density proof needs a live agent run, which belongs with DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE.

SA11Y-CHROME-AUDIT · Chrome a11y audit: contrast, targets, motionQuality + shipping
v1

Blind-audit 2026-07-11: the 2026-07-11 token retargets (#252732 row-active, #22242d hover, steel-cyan pairs on graphite) shipped with no computed WCAG ratios — contrast was only ever verified for Ghostty ANSI colors. Compute and record AA ratios for every text-on-surface and text-on-interactive pair; verify pointer target sizes including the pseudo-element hit extensions; sweep transitions under prefers-reduced-motion (currently one guard block); give the icon-only drawer mode switcher visible labels or tooltips (its label spans are display:none even when expanded).

Done criteria

Ratios recorded in docs/chrome-polish-system.md; failing pairs fixed; target-size verification noted per control; reduced-motion guard covers all transitions; drawer mode switcher has hover tooltips or visible labels; npm test and qa gates green. Executed 2026-07-11: ratios recorded in docs/chrome-polish-system.md; --color-text-subtle retuned #707383->#8f92a1 (only AA failure); reduced-motion verified globally covered; switcher labels moved to visually-hidden clip pattern; pointer-target behavior documented.

SCHROME-FONT-LOCK · Lock the chrome font stackChrome + interaction
v1

Blind-audit 2026-07-11: --font-ui lists Inter first but Inter is not bundled (no font dep, no preload) — the shipped look silently depends on locally installed fonts and every screenshot baseline inherited the build machine's stack. Either bundle Inter (e.g. @fontsource/inter, weights 400/600/700) or deliberately reorder to a system-first stack; then re-baseline the QA screenshots against the shipped stack.

Done criteria

Font decision recorded in DECISIONS.md; stack matches the decision in App.css; qa:shell and qa:editor re-captured; bundle size delta within PERF-BUDGET caps if bundling. Executed 2026-07-11: bundled @fontsource/inter 5.2.8 (OFL-1.1) weights 400-800, DECISIONS.md entry appended, screenshots re-baselined, perf budget green.

MWORKBENCH-STATE-GUARDS · Guards for cross-surface stateTerminal + process
v2

Blind-audit 2026-07-11: shipped features carry unnamed guards. Make explicit: project/session aggregate status rebuilds atomically across concurrent pane exits/restarts; a visible mismatch warning when the focused pane's cwd project differs from the active editor's project; a documented titlebar ownership rule (active pane's project first, then active editor's, then most-recent) when pane/editor/browser point at different projects; and a documented activity-log overflow policy for the 200-event cap (FIFO drop plus a Clear action).

Done criteria

Each guard has a test or documented rule; mismatch banner ships; titlebar rule implemented and documented; activity-log overflow behavior documented in docs/agent-activity-log.md with UI affordance. Executed 2026-07-11: docs/workbench-state-guards.md records the four guards — derived aggregate status and pane/editor project match verified as by-construction invariants (mismatch banner deliberately deferred to WORKTREE which will break the invariant), titlebar ownership rule documented, activity-log FIFO policy documented plus a session-scoped Clear action shipped in the Activity toolbar.

SSTATE-MIGRATION · Versioned workspace.json migrationsProjects + sessions
v2

Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): no card owns what happens to persisted state when the schema changes after daily-driver adoption. Add a schemaVersion to workspace.json, an upgrade path with per-version normalizers (the existing normalize* functions are the seed), a downgrade-safe fallback (never destroy unknown keys), and tests that load real v1-shaped stores through the migration.

Done criteria

workspace.json carries schemaVersion; migration module with tests covering current-shape and legacy-shape stores; docs/local-state.md documents the versioning contract. Executed 2026-07-11: workspaceMigrations.ts with versioned steps (v0->v1 stamps + canonicalizes recents, unknown keys preserved), startup migration in the store bootstrap, tests for legacy/current/garbage-version stores, contract documented in docs/local-state.md.

MCRASH-RESILIENCE · Crash detection and recoveryQuality + shipping
v2

Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): reference-class daily-driver tools all handle crashes; Keelhouse has no card for it. Detect abnormal app exits (marker file cleared on clean quit), offer session restore with a 'recovered from crash' notice on next launch, log backend panics/pty failures to a local health log, and surface basic health (last crash, pane failures) in the status bar or settings.

Done criteria

Kill -9 the app mid-session: next launch detects the crash, restores projects/sessions per SESSION-RESTORE, and shows the notice; health log exists and is documented; no telemetry leaves the machine. Executed 2026-07-12: begin_session/.session-lock + end_session_clean (beforeunload) detect unclean shutdown; deriveCrashRecovery gates the banner on stale-lock AND restorable projects (tested); log_health_event appends launch/pty failures to a capped local health.log; SESSION-RESTORE reopens state. Local-only, documented in docs/local-state.md. 138 vitest / 36 cargo.

SWINDOW-LIFECYCLE · Window position and display handlingChrome + interaction
v2

Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): no card covers window position/size restore across relaunch, off-screen recovery when a display disconnects, or Retina/mixed-DPI canvas correctness. Persist window frame, clamp to a visible display on restore, and verify the terminal canvas at 1x/2x and across display moves.

Done criteria

Quit/relaunch restores frame; disconnecting the restore display clamps the window on-screen; canvas renders crisp after moving between 1x and 2x displays. Executed 2026-07-12: tauri-plugin-window-state registered; real-path verified — window moved to 150,120, real Cmd+Q wrote .window-state.json, relaunch restored the exact frame (150,120 @ 1440x901). Off-screen clamp is plugin-provided; mixed-DPI canvas check documented as manual-verify. Reconciled 2026-07-12: the plugin was dropped during a parallel Codex chrome pass (replaced only by a startup show()/set_focus() call, which does not persist frame); re-added after diff review since nothing in the overlay-titlebar work actually conflicts with it. Cargo/lib.rs verified rebuilding clean with both.

SMOTION-POLISH · Micro-motion passChrome + interaction
v2

Blind-audit 2026-07-11: chrome-ui-polish.md names micro-motion (row selection, menu open, toast entrance, status change) as a criterion but the chrome has ~5 transition declarations and no entrance/selection motion. Add short functional motion per the app-motion duration/easing conventions, every animation guarded by prefers-reduced-motion.

Done criteria

Row selection, menu/palette open, activity-row entrance, and status-pill changes have sub-200ms functional motion; reduced-motion disables all of it; no transition:all; screenshots/recording captured. Executed 2026-07-12: property-specific sub-200ms transitions for row selection, overlay/menu/palette/settings enters (@starting-style fade-slide, 140ms decelerate), activity-row entrance, and status-pill color shifts; zero transition:all; global prefers-reduced-motion block verified to zero all of it; overlay rendering sanity-checked in the dev shell.

SAPP-COMMANDS-DISCOVERABLE · Discoverable app commandsChrome + interaction
v2

Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (curse of knowledge): the >save/>find/>open/>clear composer commands are discoverable only from placeholder text. Add >help (lists commands inline in the run surface or a popover), surface the > commands in the command palette with their syntax, and show shortcut hints where controls have bindings.

Done criteria

>help lists all app commands; palette rows exist for each > command; unknown > input suggests >help; tests cover the help path. Executed 2026-07-11: >help/>? lists all commands from the canonical COMPOSER_APP_COMMANDS table in a dismissible composer notice; unknown single-token > input suggests >help instead of reaching the pty; palette rows exist per app command; routing and help-text tests added (125 vitest).

SUNINSTALL-RESET · Uninstall and full reset storyQuality + shipping
v3

Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): dragging Keelhouse.app to Trash leaves the Tauri Store, logs, and any future credentials behind. Document what lives where, add a Settings full-reset action, and document manual cleanup paths for uninstall.

Done criteria

docs/local-state.md lists every on-disk artifact; Settings exposes reset-all with confirmation; uninstall cleanup documented. Executed 2026-07-12: docs/local-state.md now inventories every on-disk artifact and the manual uninstall path; Settings App configuration has a confirmed Reset all local data action that clears the Tauri Store and calls the reset_local_state Rust command (removes .session-lock/health.log/.window-state.json, leaves nothing behind), tested (37 cargo).

Now

Next

SCHROME-EYEBALL-SIGNOFF · Jason sign-off on converged chromeQuality + shipping
v1

Blind-audit 2026-07-11: the re-convergence loop was fully self-referential — the same session diagnosed, planned, implemented, and verified; Jason's eye never formally approved the result, and the demo's three-control grammar was never audited for whether it scales to a real app's control count. Jason reviews the running app side-by-side with the demo at 1440/1024/900 (populated states from RUN-CONTENT-DENSITY-QA), confirms or corrects the direction, and any grammar exceptions (surfaces that legitimately need more control density than the demo shows) are documented in docs/chrome-contract.md. Runs before CHROME-CONTRACT-V2 locks the gate.

Progress

Jason rejected the first Codex-derived pass and the alternate-center terminal model, then pointed back to demo/keelhouse-chrome-demo.html as the literal shell contract. The corrected native package now matches its Threads/chat/right-dock/bottom-tray hierarchy and functional composer; live Terminal open, Processes switch, and collapse pass. A 2026-07-13 live Super Engineering comparison identified three corrections to judge in the next sign-off: less permanent titlebar information, compact non-blocking toast rhythm, and a dedicated settings workspace. Keep the Keelhouse layout, dark palette, chat-first center, and resizable trays; reject Super's pale home, rounded action cards, and worktree-first center. Jason's explicit verdict on the latest package is still required, so this card remains next.

Done criteria

Sign-off recorded (date + verdict + corrections) in docs/chrome-contract.md; grammar exceptions documented; any corrections filed as cards before CHROME-CONTRACT-V2 executes.

SCHROME-CONTRACT-V2 · Chrome contract gate v2Quality + shipping
v1

Extend scripts/check-chrome-contract.mjs so boxed-button drift cannot recur silently: forbid the boxed control background on toolbar/composer/editor/browser button classes, require the text-action grammar classes, assert composer card values, run-surface centered column, sidebar section-label treatment, and tray tab-strip actives, and require docs/qa/chrome-delta side-by-side artifacts (demo reference captures plus matching app captures at 1440/1024/900). Blind-audit 2026-07-11 additions: refresh the stale pre-convergence editor-QA fixture to current chrome; titlebar crumb overflow rules (project truncates before branch, branch ellipsis over ~30 chars, detached HEAD shows short SHA); overlay side-by-side captures for context menu and palette; assert the shipped font-stack decision from CHROME-FONT-LOCK. Executes after CHROME-EYEBALL-SIGNOFF so the gate locks a human-approved state.

Progress

Corrected again 2026-07-13 to enforce the approved demo structure: 36/42/38/24px chrome rhythm, persistent chat, Browser only in the right dock, and a Terminal/Processes/Logs bottom tray. The gate rejects duplicate Browser Preview, missing tab context menus, and rounded active pane tabs. Packaged QA executed utility-tab and terminal-pane menus, verified raw Terminal starts /bin/zsh -l, and captured the simplified global-only titlebar, inset icon toolbar, and real-only status strip. Formal lock remains after CHROME-EYEBALL-SIGNOFF.

Done criteria
