The gate fails on reintroduction of boxed toolbar buttons (verified by a temporary revert); demo-reference PNGs are checked in and asserted; the editor fixture renders current chrome; crumb overflow rules implemented and tested with long branch names and detached HEAD; npm run qa:chrome-contract is green on the converged chrome.

MCHAT-COMPOSER-CONTEXT · Per-chat drafts and contextChrome + interaction
v1

Adapt Hashmark's useful Compose behaviors without its provider sprawl: per-chat draft/history, Up/Down recall, @file search, removable file/editor/browser context chips, paste/drop images, visible model/permission state, and accurate context payloads.

Progress

Implemented 2026-07-13: per-chat draft/history isolation, @file search, bounded readable file payloads, removable file/image chips, image paste/drop and picker cache, exact sent-context review, provider-native image inputs, and preflight rejection for outside, sensitive, binary, oversized, or unsupported attachments. Native packaged switching proved one draft does not leak and restores when returning. 197 frontend and 59 Rust tests pass. Remaining before Done: execute a packaged file-context send and image send through Codex and confirm the provider receives each exact payload.

Done criteria

Switching chats preserves independent drafts and sent history; @file attaches bounded readable content with visible removable chips; images can paste/drop with preview and removal; Enter/Shift+Enter/Escape/Up/Down remain predictable; the exact context sent to Codex is inspectable; oversized/binary/sensitive attachments fail before transmission with a clear reason.

MDAILY-DRIVER-LIVE · Timed live daily-driver runsQuality + shipping
v1

Blind-audit 2026-07-11: DAILY-DRIVER-METRICS is a readiness gate, not proof — the North Star (replace VS Code for three workflows) has never been measured live. Run the three scenarios end-to-end in the packaged/dev Tauri app with no fixtures: one-project edit+agent+preview, two-agent same-project, three-project switch/relaunch. Time pane switch, prompt send latency, and project switch; record memory/CPU baselines and compare against the equivalent VS Code session. Include one real Gemini TUI smoke since the gemini profile has never been render-verified. Define fail thresholds before running.

Progress

2026-07-12/13 packaged partial: real 4,000-line output, overlapping shell output, four-pane instrumentation, three-project switching/restoration, and the structured two-chat workflow were executed. Independent provider history, concurrent runs, selected-run cancellation within 1.8s, unaffected second-chat completion, raw-terminal boundary, and quit/relaunch restore passed. The one-project workflow now also passed: Codex made the exact one-file edit in 38.224s, the editor rendered it, a blank Shell served it in 4.183s, and the embedded Browser navigated/rendered it in 1.367s. Gemini launched in 3.603s but its prompt smoke failed API credential validation. Remaining: complete three-project relaunch timing, successful authenticated Gemini prompt/response, and controlled equivalent VS Code comparison; the first isolated VS Code attempt was not certifiable because macOS accessibility targeting selected existing windows. Evidence: docs/qa/daily-driver/live-runs.md and codex-multi-chat.md.

Done criteria

docs/qa/daily-driver/ contains timed live-run records for all three workflows with pane-switch/send/switch latencies, memory/CPU vs VS Code comparison, and a Gemini TUI render check; thresholds documented and met or failures filed as cards; STATE.md wording separates readiness evidence from live proof.

MPERF-BUDGET · Resource and jank budgetQuality + shipping
v1

Prove this is lighter than the VS Code workflow it replaces across 1-project, 2-agent, and 3-project task runs. Blind-audit 2026-07-11 addition: a concrete render-perf gate before v1 — measure IPC payload size at fast-output rates, Canvas 2D frame time at 1, 2, and 4 concurrent panes, and a jank threshold (frame-drop rate), so the deferred dirty-region/WebGL decisions trigger from data instead of surprise.

Progress

2026-07-12/13: render-perf gate built and live-verified. The original one-pane dev capture recorded 16 paints (3.8ms average, 53ms p95/max, 6.25% jank). Packaged runs add: two panes with 117 paints (1.66ms average, ~1ms p95, 63ms max, 1.71% jank; 7 IPC samples averaging 108KB) and four panes under overlapping shell output with 300 paints (~0.54ms average, 1ms p95/max, 0% jank; 220 IPC samples averaging 108KB). Inter Latin subsets keep production CSS at 87,989 bytes. A controlled Keelhouse package launch measured 1.435s and its project/browser/two-shell workload is captured. A separate extension-free VS Code process launched, but macOS accessibility targeting resolved to the user's existing VS Code windows, so equivalent integrated-terminal state was not driven or certified. Card stays next until the controlled comparison and a packaged one-pane render capture are recorded.

Done criteria

Static budgets green plus recorded render-perf numbers (IPC payload, frame times at 1/2/4 panes, jank rate) with thresholds; packaged Keelhouse vs VS Code memory/CPU comparison recorded with DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE.

MAI-CONNECTIONS · AI connections and credentials settingsTerminal + process
v2

Users need to hook up real AI tooling: Claude/Codex CLI auth, provider API keys, model defaults, MCP servers, environment variables, permission defaults, and health checks.

Progress

Foundation executed 2026-07-14: the dedicated Connections settings surface now persists typed Codex/Gemini/Claude model defaults, project environment metadata, and stdio/HTTP MCP records; provider keys, bearer tokens, and secret environment values use native macOS Keychain through backend-only commands and never enter the Tauri Store or process arguments. Full local reset removes known Keychain entries. Stdio targets resolve through the login-shell PATH and HTTP targets receive explicit format validation. Provider model defaults reach new structured chat runs. Hashmark remains the source for protocol/state-machine concepts only. Remaining before completion: backend-only secret injection into provider/raw processes, live MCP connectivity and tool discovery, OAuth PKCE/token lifecycle, explicit packaged validation, and the still-failing Gemini credential smoke.

Done criteria

Settings can configure Claude/Codex command profiles, provider/API credentials via OS-safe storage, MCP server entries, env vars, default models, permission policy, and validation checks that explain missing auth or broken connections.

MSOURCE-CONTROL-CONNECTIONS · GitHub and GitLab connectionsFiles + editor
v2

Local Git is not enough once agent work needs to move through PR/MR review. Add first-class source-hosting connections without becoming a full forge client.

Progress

2026-07-12 detection slice: Rust `source_control_status` command (cli_tool_status/extract_account_name) detects git/gh/glab presence and auth health read-only, never touching token material; Settings > Git shows a live 'git: ... · gh: ... · glab: ...' row. Verified against this machine's real state (git installed, gh installed+authenticated as jpoindexter, glab not installed). 2026-07-12 links slice: Rust `git_remote_url` (origin remote lookup) + TS `sourceControlLinks.ts` (SSH/HTTPS remote parsing, host-kind detection, GitHub vs GitLab vs self-hosted-fallback link building) power a Settings > Git > Remote row with Repo/PRs/Issues/Actions-or-Pipelines buttons opened via the existing `@tauri-apps/plugin-opener` `openUrl`. Verified: cargo test 43/43 (incl. a real git remote add/read round-trip), cargo fmt --check, npm build/test 165/165 (incl. 6 new link-builder tests against real GitHub/GitLab/self-hosted URL shapes), qa:chrome-contract; cross-checked the parser against this repo's actual origin remote (`https://github.com/jpoindexter/agent-cli.git`) and it resolves correctly. Remaining for full done: active-repo host status surfaced outside Settings (e.g. titlebar/statusbar), and OS-safe credential storage (not needed yet since gh/glab own their own credential storage today) — card stays in `next`.

Done criteria

Settings detects `git`, `gh`, and `glab` auth health; supports GitHub, GitLab.com, and self-hosted GitLab remotes; opens repo/PR/MR/issue/pipeline links in browser preview or external browser; shows source-host status for the active repo; credentials are stored only through OS-safe storage.

MAGENT-HOOKS · Built-in agent MCP/API hooksTerminal + process
v2

Give Claude/Codex-style agents a first-class way to cooperate with the app without making a plugin platform: inspect projects/panes/open files, request focus/split/new-pane actions, open diffs, and report task status through app-owned commands.

Progress

Hashmark reuse source identified 2026-07-13: app/src-tauri/src/harness.rs demonstrates tool discovery/dispatch, pending approvals, limited always-allow state, and spawned-agent attribution. Keelhouse keeps its provider adapters and appActions gate rather than copying Hashmark's second LLM tool loop. Super Engineering contributes a clearer project lifecycle vocabulary for local hooks: setup, run, pre-cleanup, teardown, and post-cleanup. Keelhouse must add preview, approval, attribution, and failure recovery before enabling those slots.

Done criteria

A local permissioned MCP/API endpoint exposes documented app-owned commands; actions that mutate files, git state, panes, or project layout require visible approval/undo where appropriate; every agent action is attributed in the UI/log.

MTERMINAL-INTL-INPUT · IME and international inputTerminal + process
v2

Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): the canvas keyboard path has never been tested with IME composition (CJK, dead keys, accented input) or non-Latin glyph fallback in the terminal grid. Verify the libghostty-vt encode path with macOS IME composition events, ensure composing text renders, and add a font-fallback chain for glyphs JetBrains Mono lacks.

Progress

2026-07-12: root cause confirmed (canvas never receives compositionstart/update/end in WebKit) and fixed — a hidden, always-focused textarea now holds terminal-pane DOM focus so composition events route correctly; positioned over the cursor cell every paint tick; compositionend sends the composed text via the existing paste path. CJK font-fallback chain added to FONT_FAMILY. Verified: npm build/test (159/159, incl. new imeCaretStyle unit tests)/qa:chrome-contract green; live tauri dev smoke test confirms normal keyboard input is NOT regressed by the canvas->textarea focus change; Enter/Return path also live-confirmed working (echo command typed+submitted+executed in a real pane via osascript keystroke, after cliclick's kp:return was isolated as broken on this machine via a clean TextEdit test, unrelated to the app). NOT verified live: a real CJK IME round-trip, and Option-dead-key composition (one attempt was inconclusive — window lost frontmost focus mid-sequence per the traffic-light chrome, not a negative result). Full writeup: docs/terminal-robustness.md. Stays in `next` until Jason confirms the two remaining live-unverified items.

Done criteria

Typing accented characters via Option-dead-keys and one CJK IME round-trip works in a real pane; missing-glyph fallback renders instead of tofu boxes; findings documented in docs/terminal-robustness.md.

SOUTSIDE-REVIEW · One outside pass before daily-driver claimsResearch & spikes
v2

Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (Johari): the project has zero outside eyes — comparative claims (Codex-level, Zed-quality) and approval-UX friction are exactly where self-assessment fails. Before publicly claiming daily-driver status, get one targeted external perspective: a Codex/Zed-familiar person reviews the chrome against the claims, or one other developer runs a real agent session and reports friction. Not a beta program.

Done criteria

One recorded outside review (notes or annotated screenshots) filed in docs/; resulting gaps triaged into cards or PARKED.

Later

LCHAT-FORK-CHECKPOINT · Chat forks and safe checkpointsTerminal + process
v2

Adapt Hashmark's session lineage and restore UX, but replace its index-mutating checkpoint implementation. Fork a chat from a message and optionally restore workspace state through a previewed, non-index-mutating snapshot path.

Done criteria

Fork from a user or assistant message creates a clearly linked chat with copied history only through that point and an independent provider thread; checkpoints never change the user's index when captured; restore previews affected files, protects dirty editor buffers, requires explicit approval, has a recovery path, and never runs unpreviewed reset --hard or clean -fd.

MCLAUDE-STRUCTURED-ADAPTER · Structured Claude CLI adapterTerminal + process
v2

Adapt Hashmark web's Claude stream-json parser behind the same provider-neutral chat event contract as Codex: resume ids, partial text, tool use/results, usage, compaction, plan updates, questions, and cancellation. Preserve Claude OAuth and never copy --dangerously-skip-permissions or prompt-in-argv behavior.

Done criteria

An authenticated Claude CLI chat starts/resumes/stops through structured events; prompts avoid argv when the installed CLI supports stdin; tool approvals use CHAT-PROVIDER-APPROVALS; usage/compaction/plan/question events render through shared chat components; adapter fixtures cover malformed/partial JSON and CLI-version capability checks.

LAGENT-ORCHESTRATION · Parallel chat orchestrationTerminal + process
v2

Adapt Hashmark's spawn-agent and parallel-dispatch concepts after independent chats are durable. A parent chat can delegate bounded tasks into linked child chats, optionally isolated by worktree, without hiding ownership or multiplying agents accidentally.

Done criteria

A user can preview and launch 2-8 linked child chats with explicit task, project/worktree, provider, model, permission, and budget; lineage and status are visible in the rail; cancel affects only selected children; results can return to the parent as attributed summaries; concurrency/resource limits, duplicate-file conflict warnings, and failure recovery are tested.

MRUN-CARDS-ADAPTER · Structured run cards via hooksTerminal + process
v2

The demo's structured thinking, working-plan, edited-file-with-Review, and approval cards, fed by real agent events through AGENT-HOOKS adapters and never inferred from terminal text. Reuses the card components shipped in RUN-SURFACE-COMPOSITION. Blind-audit 2026-07-11 addition: the boundary gets a mechanical provenance gate — an automated check that every rendered run card traces to an explicit event source (appActions/agentActivityEvents/hook payloads), so a future regex-on-terminal-text regression fails loudly; PRD carries the v1/v2 expectation caveat so the demo's structured cards are not read as a v1 promise.

Done criteria

With a hook-connected agent, real events render as demo-spec cards; without hooks, Run remains terminal-backed with no fabricated structure; a provenance test fails if any card renders from terminal-text parsing.

✅ Shipped (6)

v0

July 2026: surveyed the field (zellij, Superconductor, hashmark, cmux, Mux0, Supacode). zellij trialed then parked (static-config model, not 'open a folder'). Superconductor/hashmark/cmux evaluated in depth. All reference only — the decision is build-our-own.

Drove the build-our-own direction. Rejected options documented in PARKED.md/DECISIONS.md with reasons. ✓ 2026-07-07

hx sample.tsx next to the VSCode screenshot — confirmed a real editor with tree-sitter highlighting reads right. Informs the EDITOR slice (now CodeMirror in-app, not a TUI editor).

Done criteria

Findings logged in ERRORS.md; direction corrected. ✓ 2026-07-07

MS-SPIKE1 · libghostty-vt parsing spike — PASSEDTerminal + process
v0

spike-ghostty-vt/: real pty (portable-pty) → real 'ls -la -G' → real ANSI codes → libghostty-vt Terminal::vt_write parses → cell-by-cell readback matches exactly. Proves the terminal ENGINE choice (Ghostty's real parser in a Rust backend, not xterm.js). Caught the Zig 0.15.2 pin requirement.

Done criteria

Compiles + runs correctly, verified 2026-07-07. Logged in DECISIONS.md. ✓ 2026-07-07

LS-SPIKE2 · Tauri render + input loop — PASSEDTerminal + process
v0

spike-2/: real Tauri 2 + React/Vite + Rust app. pty → libghostty-vt → grid snapshot over IPC → Canvas 2D paint; browser key events → Ghostty encoder/compat mappings → pty. Includes Cmd+V paste, Cmd+K clear, Option word navigation/delete, mouse drag selection, and Cmd+C copy from selected canvas text.

Done criteria

npm test, npm run build, and cargo test pass. Real app UI verification covered selection/Cmd+C, Cmd+V paste, Cmd+K clear, and Option-Backspace delete-word. ✓ 2026-07-08

MS-SCAFFOLD · Tauri 2 app scaffold — folded into SPIKE-2Terminal + process
v0

The scaffold was not a separate throwaway step: SPIKE-2 proved the real Tauri 2 + React/TS/Vite frontend, Rust backend, native window, IPC events, Tauri commands, plugins, and app menu accelerator path.

Done criteria

Native app opens, IPC is wired both directions, frontend and Rust builds pass. ✓ 2026-07-08
